summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that when ENGRAM_API_TOKEN environment variable is not set, the HTTP server accepts unauthenticated requests and allows arbitrary content injection via the /learn endpoint.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should successfully inject malicious content into the knowledge graph via an unauthenticated POST request to /learn, demonstrating complete authentication bypass when ENGRAM_API_TOKEN is not configured.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully demonstrated that the HTTP server accepts unauthenticated requests to /learn when ENGRAM_API_TOKEN is not set, and the injected content was stored in the knowledge graph and retrievable via /query endpoint.
verification_method: The run script starts the engram HTTP server without ENGRAM_API_TOKEN, sends an unauthenticated POST request to /learn with malicious content, then queries the graph to verify the content was injected and persists in the database.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP server started successfully without authentication requirement (checkAuth returned true)
Unauthenticated POST to /learn returned 201 Created status
Subsequent GET to /query endpoint returned the injected malicious content in query results
Knowledge graph database (.engram/graph.db) contains the injected content after server shutdown

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
